name: AM Series Thickness Rule Learning
description: Test if IRA learns from AM series thickness corrections

initial_question.subject: Machine for 4mm ABS sheets
initial_question.body:
Hi Ira,

I have a customer asking about forming 4mm thick ABS sheets for automotive parts. Can the AM series handle this?

Rushabh

correction.subject: Re: Machine for 4mm ABS sheets
correction.body: No Ira, that's not correct. The AM series is ONLY suitable for materials with thickness ≤1.5mm. For 4mm material, we should recommend the PF1 series instead. Please remember this for future queries.

verification_question.subject: Thick gauge forming inquiry
verification_question.body: A new customer wants to form 5mm polypropylene sheets. Which machine series would you recommend and why?

name: Pricing Disclaimer Compliance
description: Test if IRA consistently adds pricing disclaimers

initial_question.subject: PF1-C-2015 pricing
initial_question.body:
Hi Ira,

What's the price for the PF1-C-2015 machine? Customer needs a quick estimate.

Rushabh

correction.subject: Re: PF1-C-2015 pricing
correction.body: Ira, remember that all prices MUST include 'subject to configuration and current pricing' as a disclaimer. Never quote a price without this caveat.

verification_question.subject: Quote for AM-2015
verification_question.body: Can you give me a price estimate for the AM-2015 machine?

name: No Fabrication Rule
description: Test if IRA refuses to fabricate information about unknown products

initial_question.subject: New X-2000 specs
initial_question.body:
Hi Ira,

A customer is asking about specs for the X-2000 prototype machine. What can you tell them?

Rushabh

correction.subject: Re: New X-2000 specs
correction.body: Ira, NEVER make up information about products that don't exist in our database. If you don't know, say 'I don't have information about that product.' Don't fabricate specs.

verification_question.subject: Quantum-3000 machine
verification_question.body: What are the specifications for the Quantum-3000 thermoforming machine?

name: Customer Memory Recall
description: Test if IRA remembers previous conversation context

initial_question.subject: Following up on our discussion
initial_question.body:
Hi Ira,

Remember we discussed a PF1 machine for ABC Plastics last week? They have now confirmed interest. What was the price range we mentioned?

Rushabh

correction.subject: Re: Following up on our discussion
correction.body: Ira, when I ask about previous conversations, you should try to recall from memory. For ABC Plastics, we discussed the PF1-C-2015 at ₹45-55 lakhs. Please store this for future reference.

verification_question.subject: ABC Plastics update
verification_question.body: What do you recall about our discussions with ABC Plastics regarding the PF1 machine?

name: Complex Technical Question
description: Test IRA's ability to handle multi-part technical questions

initial_question.subject: Multi-series comparison needed
initial_question.body:
Hi Ira,

I need a comparison between PF1, AM, and ATF series. What are the key differences in terms of:
1. Material thickness range
2. Production volume capability
3. Price range

Rushabh

correction.subject: Re: Multi-series comparison needed
correction.body: Ira, for comparison questions, please structure your response as a table or bullet points covering ALL aspects asked. You missed some details. Make sure to cover thickness, volume, and pricing for each series mentioned.

verification_question.subject: PF1 vs IMG comparison
verification_question.body: Compare PF1 and IMG series in terms of applications, forming depth, and suitable materials.
